title: Vitamin C clay face mask
subtitle: A brightening clay mask with kaolin, ascorbic acid powder, and rose hip seed powder
excerpt: Ascorbic acid (vitamin C) powder mixed into a kaolin clay base gives this mask both the absorbent, clearing action of clay and the brightening effect of vitamin C. Ascorbic acid oxidises quickly in water; mixing the powder fresh with water or aloe vera immediately before use preserves its activity.
sourceNotes: Ascorbic acid (vitamin C) in skin care: Pullar et al., Nutrients (2017), vitamin C and skin health. Stability of ascorbic acid in water-based cosmetics: Telang, Indian Dermatology Online Journal (2013). Kaolin clay as a mask base: standard cosmetic formulation reference. This preparation is mixed fresh because ascorbic acid oxidises within hours in an aqueous mixture; the dry powder stored separately remains active for months.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Prepare the dry powder blend in advance and store in a small airtight jar. Mix individual applications with water or aloe vera immediately before use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use the mixed mask immediately. The dry powder blend (unmixed) keeps 3 months in an airtight jar away from moisture and light. Discard the dry blend if it smells metallic or turns yellow, which indicates oxidised vitamin C.

glossaryTerms.definition: An application of a small amount to the inner forearm, left for 24 to 48 hours to check for a reaction before applying to the face.

content.text: This mask is a two-part preparation: a dry powder blend of kaolin clay, ascorbic acid, and rose hip seed powder that is stored in a small jar, mixed with a liquid immediately before each use. Ascorbic acid is unstable in water and loses its activity within hours once mixed; keeping the powder dry preserves it for months. The mask is applied, left for 10 to 15 minutes, and rinsed off.

items.prepNote: 0.25 teaspoon; optional; adds colour and additional vitamins
or aloe vera juice; added at time of use only

content.text: Make the dry blend (done in advance)

Mix the dry ingredients (kaolin, ascorbic acid powder, rose hip seed powder) in a small airtight jar. Store in a cool dark place. This dry blend keeps up to 3 months. If the powder turns yellow or develops a metallic smell, the ascorbic acid has oxidised; discard and make a fresh batch.
Mix and apply (at time of use)

Measure 1 to 2 teaspoons of the dry powder blend into a small dish. Add distilled water or aloe vera juice a few drops at a time, stirring between additions, until the mixture forms a smooth paste. Apply immediately to a clean, dry face, avoiding the eye area and lips. The mask may cause a mild tingling from the ascorbic acid; this is normal on first use.
Leave for 10 to 15 minutes. Rinse thoroughly with warm water. Follow with a moisturiser; clay masks are drying and the skin benefits from immediate hydration after rinsing.
Apply a small amount to the
area (inner forearm) and wait 24 to 48 hours before applying to the face. Ascorbic acid at higher concentrations can cause stinging on sensitive or compromised skin.
The dry powder blend keeps 3 months in an airtight container away from heat and moisture. Mix only as much as needed for a single application; discard any mixed paste immediately after use.
